mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor demand and future inflation pressure, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to rise as markets price in higher real rates and inflation expectations over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 135046.0 as of Feb 2013 shows ongoing recovery momentum
regime_hint: post-crisis labor recovery with accelerating employment
failure_condition: Payroll growth remains below 100,000 persons monthly for 3 consecutive months, or 10-year Treasury yield declines despite PAYEMS acceleration, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate held near zero (0.15% as of Feb 2013) signals continued monetary accommodation. Any signal of future rate lift-off or taper announcement causes immediate upward repricing of long-duration assets, raising the 10-year Treasury yield within 5 trading days as forward guidance shifts.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises above 0.25% or Fed signals taper within 20 trading days
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.15% indicates zero lower bound regime
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary policy with forward guidance sensitivity
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls despite FEDFUNDS lift-off signal, or yield response magnitude is less than 10 basis points within 5 trading days of trigger event, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signal demand strength. When both series accelerate simultaneously, equity risk appetite rises, pushing the 10-year Treasury yield upward as investors rotate into growth assets and reduce safe-haven demand for long-duration bonds over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 900k units AND RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 890k and RSAFS at 421399M as of Feb 2013 near trigger threshold
regime_hint: early-cycle demand acceleration with housing recovery
failure_condition: Both HOUST and RSAFS accelerate but 10-year Treasury yield falls, or joint signal fails to produce positive equity returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth below 0.2% month-over-month signals supply-side weakness and potential deflationary pressure. This causes the 10-year Treasury yield to fall as markets price in lower real rates and reduced inflation expectations over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth < 0.2% for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 99.4965 as of Feb 2013 shows modest growth trajectory
regime_hint: supply-constrained recovery with below-trend industrial activity
failure_condition: INDPRO weakness persists but 10-year Treasury yield rises, or yield decline is less than 15 basis points over 60 trading days, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) at 7.7% remains elevated relative to pre-crisis levels. A decline of more than 0.3 percentage points within 3 months signals accelerating labor market tightening, raising inflation expectations and causing the 10-year Treasury yield to rise as the Fed faces pressure to normalize policy.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 7.7% as of Feb 2013 provides room for rapid improvement
regime_hint: labor market tightening cycle with Phillips curve re-activation
failure_condition: UNRATE declines > 0.3pp but 10-year Treasury yield falls, or yield rise is less than 20 basis points over 60 trading days, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while FEDFUNDS remains near zero, creating a real rate squeeze. This regime mismatch forces the 10-year Treasury yield to rise sharply as markets demand compensation for eroding purchasing power and expect eventual Fed tightening within the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 232.77 and FEDFUNDS at 0.15% as of Feb 2013
regime_hint: real rate compression with inflation acceleration in zero-rate environment
failure_condition: Real rate squeeze persists but 10-year Treasury yield falls, or yield rise is less than 15 basis points within 20 trading days of trigger, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) remains below 2.5% annualized, signaling weak aggregate demand. In this low-growth regime, the 10-year Treasury yield falls as investors seek safe-haven duration and the Fed maintains accommodative policy to support growth over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate < 2.5% for 2 consecutive quarters
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13656.8B as of Q4 2012 reflects post-crisis recovery pace
regime_hint: below-trend growth with persistent output gap
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth remains below 2.5% but 10-year Treasury yield rises, or yield decline is less than 20 basis points over 60 trading days, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) accelerates above 0.5% month-over-month while unemployment (UNRATE) falls below 7.5%, signaling simultaneous supply and demand strength. This dual tightening raises inflation expectations and causes the 10-year Treasury yield to rise as the economy approaches full capacity within the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 99.4965 and UNRATE at 7.7% as of Feb 2013
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with capacity constraints emerging
failure_condition: Both INDPRO and UNRATE meet trigger conditions but 10-year Treasury yield falls, or yield rise is less than 12 basis points within 20 trading days, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.
